mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 5-year walk-forward test, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within three months signals imminent labor market deterioration and falling consumer spending, triggering negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during historical labor market soft patches.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which suppresses corporate earnings and generates negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns do not materialize when INDPRO falls below its trend during tightening periods, or the signal shows positive predictive power instead.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in core inflation below 2.5% removes upward pressure on discount rates, prompting immediate positive equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-positive in > 60% of instances where inflation drops below the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power in high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payrolls reduces aggregate income and corporate revenue growth, systematically dragging equity valuations lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no negative correlation with payroll declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures, where consumption outpaces GDP, signals strong consumer resilience that lifts equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The consumption-GDP divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals severe supply-side contraction that depresses corporate profit margins and generates negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to hold during historical manufacturing downturns.
